preFix.title: Beans on toast with cheese
preFix.subtitle: Baked beans on toast with melted cheddar on top
preFix.excerpt: Beans on toast taken one step further. Grated cheddar goes on top of the hot beans, the plate goes under a hot grill for two minutes, and the result is a bubbling, slightly crisp layer of cheese over the sauce. Simple, but better.
preFix.sourceNotes: Standard British variation on beans on toast, found in cafes, school canteens, and home kitchens. The addition of grated cheddar and a pass under the grill is the most common upgrade to the basic dish.

content.text: Beans on toast with cheese takes the original two minutes longer and is noticeably better. The cheddar melts into the sauce and picks up some colour under the grill, giving each bite a mix of creamy, sharp, and soft. Use mature cheddar for the best flavour. The bread needs to be on a grill-safe plate or tray.
Heat the grill to high. Heat the beans in a small saucepan over a medium heat, stirring, for 5 to 6 minutes until hot through.
Toast the bread, butter it, and place on a grill-proof tray or baking sheet.
Season the beans with black pepper and spoon onto the toast. Scatter the grated cheddar evenly over the top.
Slide under the hot grill for 2 to 3 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling. Watch it: it can catch quickly. Serve straight away.

items.fix: Preheat the grill to its highest setting and position the tray close to the element.
items.cause: Grill was not hot enough, or the tray was too far from the element.
items.symptom: The cheese is pale and rubbery

items.fix: Use thick-cut bread and spoon the beans on more conservatively so there is a border around the edge.
items.cause: Too much sauce spooned on, or the bread was too thin.
items.symptom: The beans overflow the toast under the grill

items.fix: Move quickly from saucepan to toast to grill. The whole process should take under 5 minutes.
items.cause: The beans sat on the toast too long before going under the grill.
items.symptom: The toast goes soggy before the cheese melts

content.text: Adding cheese to beans on toast is the most common upgrade in British home cooking, found on cafe menus and in home kitchens in equal measure. It takes the original from a two-ingredient meal to something that feels slightly more considered. Mature cheddar is the standard choice, and the British grill rather than the American broiler is what gives the top its characteristic bubble.
